title: Cyrtogonium scandens
taxonomy: Cyrtogonium scandens is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the maiden-hair ferns. This species falls under the genus Cyrtogonium, which comprises a small group of ferns native to tropical regions. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the order Polypodiales, a large group of ferns that are mostly found in tropical and subtropical areas. Cyrtogonium scandens is a species of climbing fern, which is a characteristic that distinguishes it from other members of its genus. At the kingdom level, Cyrtogonium scandens is classified as a plant, specifically within the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Cyrtogonium scandens has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves are used to make a decoction that is believed to aid in relieving coughs and colds. In some traditional medicine practices, Cyrtogonium scandens is also used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. The plant's leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Cyrtogonium scandens as a medicinal plant.
benefits: Cyrtogonium scandens, also known as the creeping fern or climbing fern, has been found to have various health benefits. It contains antioxidants and other compounds that may help protect against cell damage and inflammation. Some studies suggest that it may have anti-aging properties and help to reduce oxidative stress. The plant has also been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to confirm the extent of its health benefits and potential uses.

These compounds are known for their antioxidant properties and may contribute to the plant's potential medicinal benefits. Research is ongoing to fully understand the effects of these constituents on human health. The rhizomes of Cyrtogonium scandens are said to possess antimicrobial properties and are used to treat respiratory problems. They are also used to treat fever and other infections. preparations: Cyrtogonium scandens is a type of fern, and as such, there isn't much information available on its use in herbal preparations. However, in traditional medicine, ferns have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. side_effects: Cyrtogonium scandens, commonly known as the Rock Cap Fern, is a plant native to Hawaii, and it's known for its potential uses in traditional medicine. intro: Cyrtogonium scandens, also known as the rock cap fern, is a species of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by its delicate fronds and scrambling habit. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtogonium scandens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Cyrtogonium scandens is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis, fever, and skin irritations.
